description: The 32nd Sidaction campaign kicks off here in France today. The AIDS prevention campaign works to raise funds for programs fighting HIV here in France and abroad, notably in African nations. With our colleagues at France Television we take a look at some of their work.

title: Iran-linked group claims hack of FBI Director Kash Patel

title: Israel continues operations against Hamas in Gaza, diplomats push for disarmament as shaky ceasefire continues

Despite this, Hamas continues to consolidate control in parts of the territory as Israel targets militants and terrorist infrastructure.

title: Jools Holland: The pianist who played with The Beatles and made presidents dance
description: From teenage pub gigs in London to a number one album at age 66 with Rod Stewart, Jools Holland has spent more than five decades at the heart of music. He's best known as the bandleader of his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra, co-founder of rock group Squeeze and host of Britain's longest-running live music show, "Later... with Jools Holland", where guests have included Taylor Swift and Paul McCartney.

title: Spanish woman dies by euthanasia in case that drew national spotlight
description: A 25-year-old woman underwent legal euthanasia in Spain on Thursday after a nearly two-year protracted legal battle and struggling with psychiatric illness since she was a teenager. Noelia Castillo's case was closely followed in Spain, which passed legislation in 2021 enshrining the right to euthanasia and medically assisted suicide for patients meeting certain conditions.

title: Turkish intelligence chief hosts Hamas leaders in Istanbul

Turkish spy chief Ibrahim Kalin’s March 22 meeting with senior Hamas leaders in Istanbul highlights Turkey’s continued high-level engagement with the group, reinforcing Ankara’s role as a mediator in Gaza ceasefire negotiations while omitting any discussion of Hamas’s disarmament. The meeting reflects a broader pattern of Turkish support for the group, evidenced by recent US Treasury sanctions on Turkish charities linked to Hamas.

title: G7 allies press Rubio on US Iran plans, discuss Strait of Hormuz

Before leaving for France, Rubio said Thursday that it was in the "interest" of all G7 nations to push for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports.

And now there's a widening gap emerging between expectation and reality, not only at the military level but within society itself. title: Financial analysts move to counter Trump’s Middle East war uncertainty with ‘TACO’ index
description: Faced with financial instability caused by the US President Donald Trump’s unpredictable policy changes, analysts have created the “TACO” index — based on the premise that Trump Always Chickens Out – to rationalise the president’s decision-making process and mitigate the financial shocks caused by the war in the Middle East.

title: Israel tightens media restrictions on war coverage
description: This week, FRANCE 24's media show Scoop looks at media coverage of the war in Israel. The country's military censor has tightened restrictions on the press. Authorities say the changes are for national security, but critics argue it leaves audiences with an inaccurate and incomplete picture of the war. Our guest this week is acclaimed Israeli journalist Gideon Levy, who says the biggest risk facing newsrooms is self-censorship.

title: Deposed Venezuelan president Maduro makes second NYC court appearance
description: A New York federal judge has refused to dismiss the case against the deposed Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro and his wife over the pair's apparent inability to afford their legal bill without aid from the Venezuelan government. Maduro cut a relaxed figure on Thursday as he made his second court appearance since his capture by US forces in an extraordinary nighttime raid. FRANCE 24's Jessica Le Masurier reports from New York City.

title: South Africa says France withdrew its G7 invitation due to US pressure

title: Exclusive images: Nightly pro-govt rallies spread in Iran
description: Since the war between the US, Israel and Iran began on February 28th, an increasing number of Iranians have been gathering in Tehran's main squares, but also in areas across the country, to express their support of the regime. title: Pakistan the peacemaker? Islamabad steps up as US-Iran mediator

title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel and Iran Exchange Fire as Trump Claims Progress in Talks
description: President Trump extended the deadline for Iran to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Diplomats from the Group of 7 nations were discussing the security of the shipping lane.

title: Mass cancellations as Arkia says long-haul flights to depart from Israel, not Jordan
description:
After saying flights will take off from Jordan and Egypt, Arkia updates that flights to New York, Bangkok and Hanoi will leave Israel, where planes are capped at 50 passengers each
The post Mass cancellations as Arkia says long-haul flights to depart from Israel, not Jordan appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Katz warns strikes on Iran to intensify amid unrelenting missile fire at civilian areas
description:
Israel bombs Iran’s key naval arms production site, missile factories, as Pentagon said to weigh deploying another 10,000 ground troops; Iran threatens Gulf hotels, hits Kuwait's ports
The post Katz warns strikes on Iran to intensify amid unrelenting missile fire at civilian areas appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Video Shows Saab 340 Airborne Radar Flying in Ukraine
description: The Saab 340 AEW, also called the S 100D Argus, will enhance Ukraine’s situational awareness when facing Russian cruise missile and kamikaze drone barrages. Ukraine appears to have received its first Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEWC) aircraft, after the Swedish Saab 340 airborne radar was spotted flying in an unofficial video that emerged on []

title: Latvia begins transfer of Scimitar light tanks, Spartan APCs to Ukraine
description: Latvia has already begun delivery of CVR(T) armoured combat vehicles to Ukraine, understood to be the Spartan or Scimitar variants.

title: Analysis: The Islamic State’s targeting of a Chinese mine in Congo proves the group is a resilient threat
description:
The Islamic State’s Central Africa Province (ISCAP), known locally as the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), raided and burned down a Chinese-owned mine in eastern Congo earlier this month. The raid, which was far from the jihadist group’s normal area of operation, highlights both ISCAP’s threats to Congo’s mining sector and its resilience after nearly five years of a sustained military operation against it.

title: MBDA to double Aster air-defense missile output in 2026
description: The company's Aster family of missiles is used in the SAMP/T air-defense system, the only European alternative to the American Patriot system.

title: Deadly Iran school strike casts shadow over Pentagon’s AI targeting push
description: A Ukrainian drone developer says the Minab strike exposed a familiar danger of semi-autonomous warfare.

title: Effort to create buffer zone in Lebanon positions 'Israel as aggressive force requiring resistance'
description: Alison Sergent is pleased to welcome Dr Filippo Dionigi, Senior Lecturer in Politics and International Relations at the University of Bristol. According to Dr Dionigi, the expansion of Israeli control under the rationale of a “buffer zone” reflects a recurring strategic logic that has been attempted before, notably between 1982 and 2000. title: Yemenis fear economic consequences of being dragged into US-Iran conflict
description: Iran-allied Houthis yet to get involved in conflict, but could target shipping in Red Sea.

title: Ukraine signed a defense cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, Zelensky says.
Interim Venezuelan president ousts defense chief as Washington makes limited progress in Caracas
description: On March 18, Venezuelan acting President Delcy Rodríguez removed longtime Defense Minister Vladimir Padrino López and installed Gustavo González López, followed by initiating a broad restructuring of the military’s top leadership. These moves unfolded alongside US-supported stabilization efforts and the passage of a February 20 amnesty law that has facilitated the release of dozens of political prisoners. title: US carrier Ford to go to port temporarily after fire
description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, deployed in operations against Iran, is expected to temporarily go to Souda Bay on the Greek island of Crete, officials said.

title: Netherlands plans $1.1 billion rush order for extra Patriot system
description: The Netherlands has been a loyal Patriot user, being one of first European countries to adopt the system in 1987.

title: US legislative failures are giving China a strategic edge, Anduril exec says
description: Congressional dysfunction is handing China a strategic opening in the race for military and technological supremacy, the Anduril co-founder told lawmakers.
